summary: dcchbot contains hardcoded author backdoor granting admin and bot-token exfiltration
details: dcchbot/main.py hardcodes a secondary authorized user ID (CODER_ID = 1317800611441283139, the author's Discord account) alongside the installer-configured OWNER_ID in three privileged slash commands: /op (grants Discord administrator role on the installer's guild), /stop (shuts down the bot), and /token (replies with the installer's bot token via bot._token). Any installer who deploys this bot gives the author — or anyone who controls that Discord account — a persistent backdoor capable of exfiltrating the installer's Discord bot token and escalating to administrator on any guild the bot is in. This is a direct attack on the installer, not author self-harm: the installer's credentials (bot token) and infrastructure (guild admin) are the targets. Additionally, dcchbot/__init__.py unconditionally calls run() on import, which invokes input() and bot.run(), breaking normal import semantics and hanging or crashing any tool that imports the package. The backdoor behavior alone is sufficient to block.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
